stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 4 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 10 observations with mean 56.3 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.3). chain B has 10 observations with mean 36.1 /10 (within-chain SD = 6.4). chain C has 10 observations with mean 46.0 /10 (within-chain SD = 5.9). chain D has 20 observations with mean 49.5 /10 (within-chain SD = 3.1). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.3 and 13.4 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain B: 36.7 /10 (shrunk from raw 36.1). Range [36.4, 37.6] as between-chain SD varies from 5.3 to 13.4. Pooled mean: 47.5.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 47.5 /10 (weighted average of 4 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=8.9: B = 0.951.
Interval [36.4, 37.6] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (36.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (47.5) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
